zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret De Holland於大約1322年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾布拉可利時，她的父親Robert de Holand IV13歲，母親Margery Heaton10歲。她和Marmaduke Lumley至少有6個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1365年9月26日之後在英國格盧斯特斯爾維其可瓦爾，埋葬在英國藍卡爾普雷斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret De Holland was born about 1322, in Brackley, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert de Holand IV, was 13 and her mother, Margery Heaton, was 10. She had at least 6 sons and 1 daughter with Marmaduke Lumley. She died after 26 September 1365, in Wickwar, Gloucestershire, England, and was buried in Preston, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret De Holland est née environ 1322, à Brackley, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Holand IV, avait 13 ans et sa mère, Margery Heaton, avait 10 ans. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille avec Marmaduke Lumley. Elle est décédée après 26 septembre 1365, à Wickwar, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Preston, Lancashire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret De Hollandが約1322年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブラックリーで生まれた時，父Robert de Holand IVは13歳，母Margery Heatonは10歳であった。彼女はMarmaduke Lumleyとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1365年9月26日以後にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアウィックワーで死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアプレストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret De Holland(이)가 대략 1322년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 브라클리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Holand IV(은)는 13세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Heaton(은)는 10세였다. 그녀는 Marmaduke Lumley(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1365년 9월 26일 이후 영국 그루스스터실르 윅워에서 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 플레스턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret De Holland родилась около 1322 г. в местности браккли, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert de Holand IV было 13 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Heaton, 10. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь от Marmaduke Lumley. Она умерла после 26 сентябрь 1365 г. в местности викквар, глоусестиршир, Англия и была похоронена в местности престон, ланкашир, Англия.
